programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: With the contestants narrowed down to 32, Don Mann and Randy Couture reveal a new twist that changes the game: a complex point system that turns survival into a strategic battle. Contestants face grueling military training and a brutal 5K run through Nevada’s desert, testing their endurance and mental toughness. As mentors and adversaries, Don and Randy push the contestants to their limits. This episode marks the beginning of an intense journey where only the strongest and most strategic will prevail.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Day 4 of Season 3 kicks off with some much anticipated shooting.. or does it? This course of fire is actually using the latest cutting-edge CO2 technologies to train the contestants with another option for nonlethal force. Having a new weaponry option tucked in their arsenal, the competitors set out on their first recon mission for their CIA mission. Trudging through Alligator infested waters to their rendezvous point, the weight of the successful mission hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Contestants Stephen and Zacharias sit down to go through their experience with shooting the latest nonlethal weaponry and how their mission prep is going so far. From there, host Chris Heavens invites U.S. Arms and Athlon Optics to take a seat and show a sneak peak at their latest products. Before the end of the episode, the contestants let it slip that there’s going to be quite the surprise to come.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: For physical training, Don does something unexpected when he hands over the reins to a contestant to lead. One would think that would make for an easy go of it, if it weren’t for the 3-time Olympian that was now in charge. Next, co-host Mike Glover leads Surviving Mann’s first ever After-Action report to debrief the previous night’s mission. Afterwards, the infamous Blah line makes its season debut to the contestants’ chagrin.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Chris and Cindy sit down with Jenn and Lanny to take a deep dive into last night’s mission. If it wasn’t difficult enough to do a full day of Surviving Mann training, an overnight mission with no breaks seems to be the cherry on top. After a refreshing 2 hours of sleep, the contestants were back at it with an Olympic-level PT session hosted by Lanny. From there, Jen fills us in on a secret new product in the next episode of Surviving Mann that reminds her of... Fembots? Stay tuned.
